title: The queue at the post office
summary: Describe a quiet morning waiting in line at your local post office.
topic: The queue at the post office
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about the queue at a post office. Say where it is, who you see, what people do, and how you feel. Use present simple.
hintStarters: The post office near my flat in Bucharest is…
I stand in the queue and I look at…
sampleText: The post office near my flat in Bucharest is small and very busy. I go there on Wednesday mornings to send packages to my sister. I take a number ticket from a yellow machine at the door. Today my number is forty-six. The screen shows twenty-nine. I stand in the queue and I look around. An old man sits on a wooden bench. He holds a brown envelope on his knee. A young mother stands next to him with a sleepy child. She talks to the child in a soft voice. Two workers in blue jackets carry a heavy box to the back room. Nobody complains. Most people wait without a phone in their hand. The clock on the wall makes a loud sound. After twenty minutes, my number comes. I walk to the counter with my small package. The woman at the counter does not smile, but she works very fast. I pay and I leave. I always feel calm after a quiet queue. It is a strange kind of rest.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: gửi bưu kiện cho em gái

sampleAnnotations.phrase: without a phone in their hand

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một kiểu nghỉ ngơi kỳ lạ

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for routine and observation
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Bài này dùng hiện tại đơn để vừa kể thói quen ('I go there on Wednesday mornings') vừa mô tả những gì xảy ra trước mắt ('An old man sits on a wooden bench'). Cùng một thì, hai mục đích.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Present simple is used both for habits ('I go there on Wednesday mornings') and to describe what is happening around the writer ('An old man sits on a wooden bench').

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Articles 'a', 'an', 'the'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'a/an' khi nhắc lần đầu ('a young mother', 'a sleepy child'). Dùng 'the' khi vật/người đã biết hoặc duy nhất ('the door', 'the clock on the wall', 'the woman at the counter').
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'a/an' the first time you mention something ('a young mother', 'a sleepy child'). Use 'the' for known or unique things ('the door', 'the clock on the wall', 'the woman at the counter').

sampleTranslationVi: Bưu điện gần căn hộ tôi ở Bucharest nhỏ và rất đông. Tôi đến đó vào sáng thứ Tư để gửi bưu kiện cho em gái. Tôi lấy phiếu số từ một cái máy màu vàng ở cửa. Hôm nay số của tôi là bốn mươi sáu. Màn hình hiện số hai mươi chín. Tôi đứng trong hàng và nhìn xung quanh. Một ông cụ ngồi trên ghế gỗ. Ông cầm một phong bì màu nâu trên đầu gối. Một bà mẹ trẻ đứng cạnh ông với đứa trẻ buồn ngủ. Cô nói với đứa trẻ bằng giọng nhẹ nhàng. Hai nhân viên mặc áo khoác xanh khiêng một cái thùng nặng vào phòng sau. Không ai phàn nàn. Hầu hết mọi người đợi mà không cầm điện thoại. Đồng hồ trên tường kêu rất lớn. Sau hai mươi phút, đến số tôi. Tôi đi đến quầy với bưu kiện nhỏ. Người phụ nữ ở quầy không cười, nhưng làm việc rất nhanh. Tôi trả tiền và đi ra. Tôi luôn cảm thấy bình yên sau một hàng đợi yên tĩnh. Đó là một kiểu nghỉ ngơi kỳ lạ.

mcQuestions.prompt: When does the writer go to the post office?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I go there on Wednesday mornings'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the day in the second sentence.

mcQuestions.prompt: What is the writer's number today?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'Today my number is forty-six'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the old man hold?
options: A brown envelope on his knee
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'He holds a brown envelope on his knee'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the old man's action.

mcQuestions.prompt: How does the young mother talk to the child?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'She talks to the child in a soft voice'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What do most people in the queue do?
options: They wait without a phone in their hand.
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'Most people wait without a phone in their hand'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is in the present simple, describing the scene?
options: An old man sat on a wooden bench.
An old man will sit on a wooden bench.
An old man sits on a wooden bench.
An old man sitting on a wooden bench.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple is used to describe what is happening around the writer.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which article is correct: 'I pay and I leave ___ post office.'
mcQuestions.explanation: We already know which post office it is, so use 'the'.
mcQuestions.hint: We have already talked about this post office.

options: An young mother stands next to him.
An old man sits on a wooden bench.
An brown envelope is on the bench.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'an' is used before a vowel sound: 'an old man'.

mcQuestions.prompt: How long does the writer wait?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'After twenty minutes, my number comes'.

mcQuestions.prompt: How does the writer feel after the queue?
options: Calm — a strange kind of rest
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I always feel calm after a quiet queue. It is a strange kind of rest'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look at the last two sentences.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why can quiet waiting in a public place sometimes feel restful, even when we 'lose' time?
